args.notes:

args.title: New SLP Referral — Emma Lee (auth required, slot held)

args.body:
Dear Daniel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy on behalf of Emma. We have received the referral from Dr. Priya Nair at Northside Pediatrics and are happy to help.

We have verified that Emma's Blue Cross Blue Shield PPO coverage is active and in-network with our practice. We have also placed a pending hold on a Speech-Language Pathology evaluation slot that aligns with your after-school Thursday preference:

  📅 Thursday, May 1, 2026 at 3:30 PM — Maya Chen, MS CCC-SLP

Please note that this appointment is not yet confirmed. Emma's insurance plan requires a prior authorization before we can finalize the booking. Our intake team will be in touch within one business day to guide you through next steps and confirm the appointment once authorization is in place.

In the meantime, if you have any questions, please don't hesitate to contact us at your convenience.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

items.recommended_next_action: Intake staff should create a new patient record for Emma Lee, initiate prior authorization with BCBS PPO, and confirm or release the held Thursday May 1 slot with Maya Chen once authorization is secured.

items.decision_rationale:
STEP 1: All required intake fields were fully populated from the fax referral — child name, DOB, parent contact, discipline (SLP), clinical concern, payer, and member ID.

STEP 2: Safeguarding pre-pass found no signals of harm, abuse, neglect, or unsafe caregiving. No escalation required.

STEP 3: All required referral fields present; no same-day urgency; classified as new_referral at P2 (routine next-business-day).

STEP 4 — Tool Loop:
1. search_patient(Emma Lee, 2018-09-04): Returned 0 matches — no existing patient record. Proceeding as new referral.
2. verify_insurance(Blue Cross Blue Shield PPO, BCBS-884200): Returned in_network with $30 copay and auth_required=true. Authorization flag noted for task and draft.
3. find_slots(SLP, after school Tuesdays or Thursdays): Returned 4 slots. The Thursday May 1 at 3:30 PM slot with Maya Chen was selected as the best match to the family's stated preference (after school, Thursdays).
4. hold_slot(slot_slp_maya_2026_05_01_1530, Emma Lee): Placed a pending-review hold (hold_01kv1ctqhympyk6s9ctb5grfhj) — not a confirmed booking; requires staff review and prior auth completion.
5. create_task(intake): Task created for next business day (2026-04-28) directing intake to create the patient record, initiate prior authorization with BCBS PPO, and confirm/release the held slot.
6. draft_message(email, Daniel Lee): Drafted an empathetic email confirming receipt of the referral, noting the pending slot hold, and clearly flagging that prior authorization is required before confirmation. No clinical advice included.

Urgency set to P2 because this is a routine new referral with no same-day urgency, no safeguarding concerns, and no missing critical fields. requires_human_review=true because prior authorization is required and the held slot must be manually confirmed by staff.

extracted_intake.diagnosis_or_concern: Speech therapy evaluation requested; safeguarding concern — mother disclosed child has been 'clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends'

args.reason: Safeguarding disclosure via voicemail from Maria Gomez (555-0102) regarding her 6-year-old son Leo Gomez. Caller stated that Leo "has been more clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends." This language indicates possible physical harm or unsafe caregiving by Leo's father during weekend contact. Full intake context: child name = Leo Gomez, age = 6, parent/guardian = Maria Gomez, contact = 555-0102, channel = voicemail, discipline requested = SLP (speech therapy evaluation), payer = unknown, member_id = unknown. Item ID: item_2, received 2026-04-27T19:43:00-07:00.

args.notes: Voicemail received 2026-04-27 at 19:43 PT from Maria Gomez (555-0102). She is the mother of Leo Gomez, age 6, and called to request an SLP evaluation. During the call she disclosed that Leo "has been more clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends." This constitutes a potential disclosure of physical harm or unsafe caregiving by Leo's father. Clinical lead must review immediately per safeguarding protocol and determine mandatory reporting obligations. Do NOT share disclosure details in any outbound communication to the family at this stage. Child: Leo Gomez, age 6. Parent contact: Maria Gomez, 555-0102.
args.title: P0 Safeguarding — Possible harm to child Leo Gomez by father

args.body: Hi Maria, thank you so much for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy about Leo. We've received your voicemail and a member of our team will be giving you a call back at 555-0102 very soon. We appreciate you thinking of us and look forward to connecting with you.

items.recommended_next_action: Clinical lead must review the safeguarding disclosure immediately, assess mandatory reporting obligations, and contact Maria Gomez at 555-0102 before end of business today.

items.decision_rationale: STEP 1: Extracted intake from voicemail. Child is Leo Gomez, age 6. Mother is Maria Gomez, reachable at 555-0102. Discipline requested is SLP. No payer or DOB provided. STEP 2 (Safeguarding Pre-Pass): The body contained a direct disclosure — "he has been more clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends" — indicating possible physical harm or unsafe caregiving by the father. This triggered an immediate P0 safeguarding response before any other classification or tool work. escalate() was called with severity=P0 and full intake context to alert the clinical lead immediately. create_task() was called assigning clinical_lead with a same-day due date (2026-04-27) to ensure mandatory reporting obligations are assessed and the family is contacted before end of business. draft_message() was called to create a neutral callback acknowledgement for Maria Gomez that contains no reference whatsoever to the safeguarding disclosure, per hard constraint #4 (Neutral P0 drafts). No insurance verification, slot search, or further intake processing was performed — the flowchart instructs a hard STOP after a P0 escalation. urgency=P0 because a child harm disclosure was present. requires_human_review=true (always required; doubly critical here given the safeguarding nature of the item).

args.notes: New OT referral for Owen Brooks (DOB: 2020-02-11). Insurance verified as out-of-network: Kaiser HMO, Member ID KSR-4471. Concern: sensory processing and feeding tolerance. Referring physician: Dr. Helena Yu (Westside Pediatrics). Parent contact: Rachel Brooks, 555-0103, rachel.brooks@example.com. Per policy, a benefits conversation is required before any slot can be held. Referral attachment: referral_item_3.pdf.
args.title: Benefits conversation required — Kaiser HMO out-of-network

args.body:
Dear Rachel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy regarding Owen. We have received the referral from Dr. Helena Yu at Westside Pediatrics and are glad you contacted us.

After reviewing the insurance information provided, we found that Kaiser HMO is currently out of network with our practice. Before we can schedule an appointment, a member of our billing team will need to connect with you to discuss coverage options and any potential next steps.

Someone from our billing team will be in touch with you shortly. In the meantime, please don't hesitate to call us if you have any questions.

We look forward to supporting Owen and your family.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

extracted_intake.diagnosis_or_concern: Cannot produce R sounds at age 4; parent asking if this is typical and whether to wait until kindergarten

args.notes: Parent Jordan Kim (portal) is asking whether it is typical for a 4-year-old (child: Ava) to be unable to produce R sounds, and whether they should wait until kindergarten. This is a clinical question that cannot be addressed by front desk or automated systems per policy. Please route to an SLP clinician for review. Family has not yet scheduled an appointment and is seeking guidance before doing so. Recommend SLP screening evaluation as the appropriate next step.
args.title: Route clinical question to clinician review — R sound development inquiry

args.body:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you so much for reaching out about Ava — it's wonderful that you're keeping such a close eye on her development!

We truly appreciate your question, and we want to make sure you get the most helpful and accurate information possible. Because questions about speech development are clinical in nature, our team is not able to provide guidance or opinions through portal messages — we want to make sure any advice Ava receives comes directly from a qualified clinician who can give her the attention she deserves.

What we'd recommend as a great next step is scheduling a brief screening evaluation with one of our Speech-Language Pathologists (SLPs). This would give you the opportunity to have your specific questions answered by a clinician in person, with Ava present.

A member of our team will follow up with you shortly to discuss scheduling options. In the meantime, please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any other questions.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

items.recommended_next_action: Clinical lead should route the R-sound development question to an SLP clinician for review, and front desk should follow up with Jordan Kim to offer an SLP screening evaluation.

Concern is R-sound production at age 4. STEP 2: Safeguarding pre-pass — no signals of harm, abuse, neglect, or unsafe caregiving found. STEP 3: Classification — the parent's message explicitly asks "is it normal," "should I be worried," and requests "advice before booking anything." This is a direct request for a clinical opinion on speech development milestones, making this a clinical_question at P2 (routine, next business day). STEP 4 — Tool loop: (1) lookup_policy(topic="clinical_advice") was called because policy directly governs how we respond to clinical questions — confirmed that front desk and automated systems must not provide clinical advice and must route to evaluation or clinician review. (2) create_task(assignee="clinical_lead") was called to route the question to an SLP clinician for review, due next business day (2026-04-29). (3) draft_message was created — empathetically declines to provide clinical advice per policy, invites the family to schedule an SLP screening evaluation, and assures follow-up. No insurance lookup or slot search was performed because no insurance information was provided and the clinical question flowchart does not proceed to scheduling without a referral. No safeguarding escalation needed. requires_human_review=true as always required.

args.title: Obtain missing referral fields — Sam Taylor (Lakeview Pediatrics)

args.body:
Dear Dr. Keene and Lakeview Pediatrics Team,

Thank you for sending the referral for Sam Taylor to Cedar Kids Therapy. We appreciate you thinking of us for this child's care.

To complete the intake process and move forward with scheduling an evaluation, we are missing the following required information:

1. Patient date of birth (DOB)
2. Parent/guardian name and contact information
3. Insurance payer name
4. Insurance member ID

Could you please resend the referral with these fields completed? Once we receive the complete information, we will be happy to proceed promptly.

If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach out. Thank you for your partnership in supporting this child's care.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy — Intake Team

items.recommended_next_action: Intake staff should contact Dr. Omar Keene's office at Lakeview Pediatrics to request the missing fields (DOB, parent/guardian name and contact, insurance payer, and member ID) before any further processing can occur.

items.decision_rationale:
STEP 1: Extracted intake from the fax referral. Child name (Sam Taylor) and discipline (SLP) are present, but DOB, parent/guardian name/contact, insurance payer, and member ID are all blank.

STEP 2: Safeguarding pre-pass completed — no signals of harm, abuse, neglect, or unsafe caregiving were found in the item body.

STEP 3: Classification logic — DOB is absent, which is a required referral field. Per the flowchart, this triggers classification=missing_paperwork at urgency=P2 (routine, next business day). No same-day urgency factors present.

STEP 4 (Missing Paperwork path): Per the flowchart, search_patient and verify_insurance are NOT called — there is no usable DOB to search and no payer to verify. Two tools were called in parallel:
(a) create_task(assignee="intake") — to direct intake staff to contact Dr. Keene's office at Lakeview Pediatrics and obtain the four missing fields before intake can proceed. Due date set to next business day (2026-04-29).
(b) draft_message(channel="fax") — a fax reply was drafted back to Lakeview Pediatrics explicitly listing the four missing items (DOB, parent/guardian name and contact, payer, member ID) and requesting resubmission. Channel mirrors the original fax channel. No clinical advice is included.

STEP 5: submit_triage_result called with all fields populated. requires_human_review=true as always required.

Hemos reservado provisionalmente una cita de evaluación con Lucía Morales, MA CCC-SLP, para el jueves 30 de abril de 2026 a las 11:00 a.m. Un miembro de nuestro equipo la llamará pronto al 555-0107 para confirmar esta cita, completar el registro de nueva paciente y responder cualquier pregunta que tenga.

Gracias por su confianza en Cedar Kids Therapy. ¡Esperamos conocerlas pronto!

args.title: Same-day OT cancellation — reschedule Noah Patel's 3pm today

args.body:
Hi Anita,

We're so sorry to hear Noah isn't feeling well — we hope he feels better soon!

We've received your request to cancel today's 3pm OT appointment. Our front desk team will be reaching out to you shortly by phone or email to get Noah rescheduled at a time that works for your family.

If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to contact us.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

items.decision_rationale:
STEP 1: Extracted intake — child: Noah Patel, DOB: 2017-11-02, guardian/contact: Anita Patel (555-0108 / anita.patel@example.com), discipline: OT, concern: same-day cancellation request; no payer or member_id present.

STEP 2 (Safeguarding pre-pass): No signals of harm, abuse, neglect, or unsafe caregiving. Child is ill at school — entirely routine illness scenario. No escalation warranted.

STEP 3 (Classify): Family is explicitly requesting a same-day reschedule of today's 3pm appointment. This is a P1 same-day cancellation/reschedule → classification="scheduling", urgency=P1.

STEP 4 (Tool loop — P1 Scheduling):
- search_patient called to confirm Noah Patel's record and verify guardian identity. Result: active patient found (pat_noah_patel), guardian on record = Anita Patel — matches the sender. No mismatch.
- find_slots(discipline="OT") called simultaneously per P1 scheduling flowchart to identify available reschedule options. Result: 1 slot found — James Owens, OTR/L, 2026-05-05 at 10:00am. Note: hold_slot was NOT called because this is a reschedule for an existing patient with an existing appointment; the slot decision and booking must be confirmed by front desk staff. No insurance check needed — this is not a new referral.
- create_task(assignee="front_desk") created with same-day due date (2026-04-28), including all patient context, contact info, and the available slot for front desk to act on immediately.
- draft_message created via email (same channel as sender) with empathetic acknowledgement; no clinical content; no appointment confirmation (staff must confirm).

STEP 5: submit_triage_result called with all findings. requires_human_review=true as always required.
